stories.body: Two powerful earthquakes measuring magnitude 7.5 and 7.2 struck near Morón, west of Caracas, killing at least 32 people and injuring about 700 as buildings collapsed across the capital, forcing airport, metro and rail closures while acting president Delcy Rodríguez declared a state of emergency, urged evacuations and mobilized nationwide search-and-rescue teams as the USGS warned of high casualties and extensive damage.
stories.headline: Twin earthquakes leave Venezuela mourning dozens amid massive rescue push

stories.body: US Central Command said American forces destroyed Iranian coastal radar installations at Goruk and Qeshm Island after downing four Iranian drones targeting Strait of Hormuz shipping, while President Donald Trump claimed Tehran retains roughly 22% of its missile arsenal even as Iran demanded Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon and conditioned any broader cease-fire with Washington on halting the parallel war involving Hezbollah.
stories.headline: US hits Iranian radar sites after drone attacks as Tehran links any truce to Lebanon

stories.body: NASA mission control directed five astronauts into their Crew Dragon for two hours after an air leak aboard Russia’s Zvezda module worsened from one pound of air per day to two, reflecting a dispute with Roscosmos over using a saw to reach a crack before Russian engineers sealed one of two leaks and paused further work, allowing the seven-member crew to resume normal operations while agencies reassess long-term fixes.
stories.headline: NASA orders brief safe-haven as ISS air leak doubles before Roscosmos pauses repairs

stories.body: Kyiv authorities said Russian barrages ignited fires in the Podil district, torched cars in Obolon and damaged a nine-story apartment block as residents sheltered underground, prompting President Volodymyr Zelenskiy to repeat intelligence warnings that Moscow is preparing a larger strike after vowing retaliatory “systematic” attacks on Ukrainian leadership sites.
stories.headline: Russia pounds Kyiv with missiles as Zelenskiy warns of looming massive strike

stories.body: Former prime minister Ousmane Sonko said Pastef will not join President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s next cabinet after disagreements over the party’s role despite its parliamentary dominance, exacerbating political turmoil as lawmakers reinstated Sonko as speaker and the government faces a suspended $1.8 billion IMF program following hidden-debt revelations that pushed liabilities to 132% of GDP.
stories.headline: Senegal’s ruling majority fractures as Pastef quits government amid IMF talks

stories.body: In a 52-47 pre-dawn vote, Senate Republicans approved an extra $70 billion for Homeland Security immigration enforcement without Democratic support and rejected attempts to permanently kill Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, setting up a House fight as moderates warn the unspent account, reportedly frozen by DOJ, could still reimburse Trump allies who claim government mistreatment.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding bill while leaving Trump’s controversial fund intact

stories.body: After sweeping several New York primaries, Democratic Socialists of America launched a nationwide survey of its 250 chapters to identify a 2028 presidential candidate, insisting even Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez would need grassroots approval while leaders debate whether to back a familiar figure or elevate new voices as they aim to channel millions of volunteer hours into reshaping the Democratic Party’s agenda.
stories.headline: Democratic Socialists chart plan to field their own 2028 presidential contender

stories.body: A Politico review found Arizona Sen. Ruben Gallego repeatedly used campaign and leadership PAC funds for family travel to Disney parks, Miami, Chicago and St. Barts, reimbursed more than $18,000 in child care since 2019 and co-hosted a donor-funded 2023 Super Bowl outing with Eric Swalwell, expenditures his aides say complied with FEC rules but that critics argue resemble a personal slush fund as he weighs higher office.
stories.headline: Spending scrutiny dogs Sen. Ruben Gallego amid talk of a 2028 bid

stories.body: A POLITICO/Public First survey found 44% of US adults think wagering on election outcomes should be illegal and most would not participate in prediction markets even as platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket process hundreds of millions of dollars in bets, fueling regulatory debates over insider trading risks, democratic integrity and whether federal or state authorities should oversee the rapidly expanding industry.
stories.headline: Poll shows Americans uneasy with political betting despite booming prediction markets

stories.body: State Attorney General Alan Wilson defeated Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette in South Carolina’s Republican gubernatorial runoff despite Donald Trump’s initial backing of Evette, after the former president issued an unusual dual endorsement to avoid another loss, helping the 52-year-old prosecutor and son of Rep. Joe Wilson secure the nomination to face Democrat Jermaine Johnson in a race Republicans are favored to win.
stories.headline: Alan Wilson wins South Carolina GOP runoff after Trump hedges endorsements

stories.body: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez told Congress that corruption cases tied to expelled PSOE figures such as José Luis Ábalos and Koldo García do not reflect widespread rot, urging partners to resist opposition calls for elections while parties like ERC, PNV and Junts questioned the government’s direction and reiterated there is no parliamentary majority to replace him despite growing tension.
stories.headline: Sánchez rejects claims of systemic corruption as allies demand a refreshed agenda

stories.body: The National Police’s UDEF unit told the Plus Ultra judge it believes former prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero covertly earned €200,000 via Focus Social Research to influence Bolivia on behalf of Peru’s Grupo Gloria and avoid a $107 million fine, allegations his circle insists were legitimate consulting, while his lawyers consider lawsuits after investigators circulated his full 2024–25 agendas and private messages.
stories.headline: Police report links Zapatero to €200,000 Bolivian lobbying fee as he mulls legal action over leaks

stories.body: Madrid judge Juan Carlos Peinado compelled Prime Minister Sánchez’s wife Begoña Gómez to hand over her passport, stay in Spain and check in biweekly while she faces charges including influence peddling, corruption and malversation, justifying the move by suggesting her police escorts could aid a getaway—comments now under disciplinary review as Gómez appeals the restriction and Sánchez decries excessive measures.
stories.headline: Judge orders Begoña Gómez to surrender passport, sparking backlash over flight-risk claims

stories.body: Spain’s anti-corruption prosecutors requested six years for continuous bribery and two for criminal-group membership against former Socialist deputy Juan Bernardo “Tito Berni” Fuentes Curbelo, while alleged fixer Marco Antonio Navarro faces 13 years including counts of fraud, forgery and influence trafficking for a 2020–21 scheme in the Canary Islands involving businessmen and officials.
stories.headline: Prosecutors seek eight-year sentence for ex-PSOE lawmaker ‘Tito Berni’ in Mediator case

stories.body: Catalan police are investigating a tip about shots fired inside a Born district apartment during Sant Joan festivities, but officers found no bullet impacts, shell casings or victims and say firecracker noise may have prompted confusion while residents refuse to cooperate and detectives seek witnesses as they also examine whether two nearby cocaine-related arrests are connected.
stories.headline: Mossos probe unconfirmed Sant Joan shooting report in El Born

stories.body: Barcelona’s PM10 concentrations soared overnight during the Sant Joan revelry, compounding an existing Saharan dust intrusion and producing hazy, humid “boirum” conditions even though hourly peaks were slightly lower than past years, prompting the Catalan government to maintain a preventive pollution alert as forecasts warn stagnant air could trigger a formal episode in coming days.
stories.headline: Sant Joan bonfires and Sahara dust push Barcelona particle pollution to dangerous highs

stories.body: Tenants at 51 Sant Antoni Abat, some with four decades in the building, say the new owner is refusing to renew leases and offering unaffordable purchase options of about €200,000 for 40-square-meter flats, forcing families with deep social ties to the neighborhood to organize with the Raval Rebel Assembly, oppose 30-day vacate orders delivered by burofax and demand mediation promised by City Hall.
stories.headline: Thirteen Raval families resist eviction from long-term rentals on Sant Antoni Abat

stories.body: Multiple infrastructure incidents, including a fire near Granollers-Canovelles and track failures on the coastal corridor, suspended or single-tracked trains on the R2, R2 Nord, R3, R4 and R11 lines for hours before traffic was gradually restored, leaving commuters facing severe delays while operators urged riders to monitor live updates for lingering disruptions.
stories.headline: Cascading Rodalies faults snarl R2, R3, R4 and R11 services
